rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LILLIAN WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Special Demurrer To Petition For Order Requiring Return Of Estate Property Pursuant To Probate Code 850
rulings.Rulings: THIS TENTATIVE RULING IS POSTED LATE 6/11/2024 AT 3:16 P.M. PLEASE SEE LOCAL RULE 14.10 (part 1 of 4) This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Respondent Ray Torres' Special Demurrer to the Petition for Order Requiring Return of Estate Property Pursuant to Probate Code SEC 850, filed by Petitioner Nelson Wong, individually and in his capacity as personal representative of the Estate of Lillian Wong. Respondent specially demurs on the ground that there is another action pending between the same parties on the same cause of action. (C.C.P. SEC 430.10(c).) For the reasons that follow, Respondent's Special Demurrer is OVERRULED. On September 27, 2022, Nelson Wong (Decedent's brother, now deceased himself) filed a petition for probate in the instant action, (the "Estate action"). On November 14, 2022, Decedent's long-term partner, Ray Torres ("Respondent"), filed an Opposition/Objection to Nelson Wong's Petition for Letters of Administration, and brought suit pursuant to Probate Code SEC 850 in the matter of In Re The California Revocable Living Trust of Lillian Wong, PTR-22-305818, (the "Trust action"). Respondent's Petition to Confirm Real Property Listed on Schedule Attached to Trust as Trust Asset (the "Trust Petition"), seeks to confirm the validity of Decedent's trust and transfer real property-22 Cameo Way, San Francisco-to Respondent, as trustee. On September 13, 2023, Nelson Wong was appointed Special Administrator of the Estate. On November 1, 2023, Nelson Wong, individually and in his capacity as personal representative, filed his own 850 petition in the Estate action. The Petition for Order Requiring Return of Estate Property Pursuant to Probate Code SEC 850 (the "Estate Petition"), seeks the return of estate assets allegedly misappropriated by Respondent. Incidentally, Nelson Wong passed away on March 27, 2024. On April 24, 2024, this Court appointed Nelson Wong's wife, Joyce Wong, Special Administrator of the Estate of Lillian Wong, to represent the estate in these various litigated matters. Additionally, Joyce Wong, as Special Administrator of the Estate of Nelson Wong, was also substituted in place of Nelson Wong, individually, in the Estate matter and in the Trust matter, and deemed a party to the actions in that capacity. On April 5, 2024, Respondent filed the instant demurrer. Respondent asserts that abatement of the Estate action is warranted because his Petition to Confirm Real Property Listed on Schedule Attached to Trust as Trust Asset, filed November 14, 2022, in the Trust action and the Petition for Order Requiring Return of Estate Property Pursuant to Probate Code SEC 850, filed on November 1, 2023, and amended on December 4, 2023, are duplicative and involve the same parties and the same causes of action. Petitioner opposes. (see part 2 of 4)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LILLIAN WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Special Demurrer To Petition For Order Requiring Return Of Estate Property Pursuant To Probate Code 850 (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling.
rulings.Rulings: (part 3 of 4) In regard to the petition in the instant case, the claims made by Nelson Wong, individually and in his capacity as Special Administrator, seek to recover estate assets allegedly misappropriated by Respondent. The Estate Petition alleges that the real property at issue belongs to the Estate, and requests an interim order for possession or FMV resolution of Respondent's petition in the Trust action. In contrast, the Trust Petition involves a determination of the validity of the purported Trust and the trustee's intent as to that specific property. Accordingly, the underlying factual allegations alleged in each petition and the proof needed to support each action differ in this respect. More importantly, however, even if Respondent prevails and the real property is deemed an asset of the Trust, it will not give rise to res judicata or claim preclusion. The Estate Petition is more extensive and seeks to recover other estate assets including, allegedly misappropriated funds from estate bank accounts, funds obtained through the use of allegedly fraudulent beneficiary forms, and the cessation of use of Decedent's vehicle. Respondent argues in reply that the Trust Petition also seeks the personal property sought in the Estate Petition by pointing to the trust provision conveying Decedent's personal property to Respondent. However, Probate Code sec 851 Notice of Hearing requires "a description of the property that is the subject of the sec 850 petition sufficient to provide adequate notice to any party who may have an interest in the property." (Id. at subd. (c)(1).) For real property, the notice must include the street address or a description of the property's location and assessor's parcel number. (Ibid.) Consistent with the narrow relief sought in his petition, Respondent's Notice of Hearing contains only a reference to the real property located at 22 Cameo Way. Neither the vehicle, bank accounts, pension, nor life insurance benefits were included, addressed, or the subject of the Notice of Hearing or the Trust Petition. Nor do the actions involve identical parties, since the Estate of Lillian Wong is not a party to the Trust action. While Nelson Wong, individually, has appeared as both a respondent and petitioner in the Trust action, the Estate of Lillian Wong has not. Notably, Respondent does not dispute in reply that the Estate has never been served with the Trust Petition and has never appeared in that matter. For the foregoing reasons, the demurrer is overruled. The Cout does not find that the pending matters involve the same parties or the same cause of action. Petitioner shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. (see part 4 of 4)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LILLIAN WONG

rulings.Rulings: (part 4 of 4) Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. (end of tentative ruling).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LILLIAN WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Special Demurrer To Petition For Order Requiring Return Of Estate Property Pursuant To Probate Code 850 (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling).
rulings.Rulings: (part 2 of 4) rent pending A plea in abatement pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 430.10, subdivision (c), may be made by demurrer or answer when there is another action pending between the same parties on the same cause of action. (Plant Insulation Co. v. Fibreboard Corp. (1990) 224 Cal.App.3d 781, 787.) The existence of the other lawsuit may not appear on the face of the complaint. But the court can take judicial notice of the court files in the same court, or in other courts, (Evid. Code, SEC 452), and matters so noticed can be considered as ground for demurrer. (C.C.P. SEC 430.30(a); see, Bistawros v. Greenberg (1987) 189 Cal.App.3d 189, 191-192.) Respondent's request for judicial notice of the (1) Petition to Confirm Real Property Listed on Schedule Attached to Trust as Trust Asset, filed November 14, 2022, in the Trust action, and (2) Petition for Order Requiring Return of Estate Property Pursuant to Probate Code SEC 850, filed on November 1, 2023, in the Estate action, is granted. (Evid. Code, SEC 452.) On Reply, Respondent requests judicial notice of his civil complaint filed in Case No. CGC-24-613951. While this item constitutes a court record, it is not relevant to the outcome of this demurrer. (See, Gbur v. Cohen (1979) 93 Cal.App.3d 296, 301 [judicial notice is confined to those matters which are relevant to the issue at hand]; see also, Evid. Code SEC 452 ["Judicial notice may be taken of the following matters ." (emphasis added)].) The fact that the Probate Court is "already and currently addressing" the same dispute raised by the civil action is immaterial to resolving the narrow issue raised in the instant motion-whether the Court should abate the Estate action until the Trust action is adjudicated. Accordingly, the request is denied. The plea in abatement requires absolute identity of parties, causes of action or remedies sought in the initial and subsequent actions. (Plant Insulation Co., supra, 224 Cal.App.3d at 788.) Regarding absolute identity of causes of action, "the rule is that such a plea may be maintained only where a judgment in the first action would be a complete bar to the second action." (Id. at 787-788.) The underlying theory of the plea of another action pending is that the first action will normally be an ample remedy, and the second action (here, the Estate action) is therefore unnecessary and vexatious. (Williams v. State of California (1976) 62 Cal.App.3d 960, 964.) Issue preclusion or collateral estoppel is not enough; identical causes of action must be involved in both suits, so that a judgment in the first action would be res judicata on the claim in the other action. (See Bush v. Superior Court (1992) 10 Cal.App.4th 1374, 1384.) Here, a review of Respondent's Trust Petition reveals that Respondent seeks solely to "confirm" that the real property located at 22 Cameo Way is an asset of the purported trust. In support of the petition, Respondent includes a copy of the California Revocable Living Trust of Lillian Wong, with a schedule attached identifying and listing the real property as a trust asset. No other relief is sought, and no other assets are mentioned. The petition does not request "the transfer of all of Lillian's property to [Respondent]," contrary to Respondent's assertions in his moving papers. (see part 3 of 4)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JOHNNY J. AND MARGIE CHERRY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Order Imposing Terminating Sanctions Evidence Sanctions Issue Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling).
rulings.Rulings: [PART 2 of 2] Monetary sanctions are awarded under CCP sections 2031.310(h) and 2031.310(i), based on both Respondents' failure to obey the Court's January 24, 2024 orders, and unsuccessful opposition to the instant motion, without a showing that such an imposition would be unjust. Respondents Jimmy Cherry and Delphine Cherry and the law firm of Clinton Killian, Attorney at Law, APC are jointly and severally ordered to pay Petitioner $2,497.50 in monetary sanctions within 5 calendar days of the May 15, 2024 hearing. (Sanctions award amount calculated based on Decl. of Robinson ISO Motion at par. 9.) Petitioner's request for terminating sanctions is hereby continued to June 12, 2024 at 2:30 p.m. to give Respondents one final opportunity to comply with this Court's directives and properly respond to discovery. To comply with this Court's directives, Respondents must: " Pay the previously sanctioned amount of $5,242.50 to Petitioner by May 20, 2024. " Pay the new sanctions in the amount of $2,497.50 to Petitioner by May 20, 2024. " Serve Delphine Cherry's responses to Special Interrogatories Set 1 and Set 2, without objections, in her individual and in her official capacity. These responses must be verified and code compliant and served no later than June 4, 2024. " Serve Jimmy Cherry's responses to Special Interrogatories Set 1 and Set 2, without objections, in his individual and in his official capacity. These responses must be verified and code compliant and served no later than June 4, 2024. " Produce all documents in response to Petitioner's Request for Production of Documents Set 1. Documents shall be produced no later than June 4, 2024. " Re-file the Declaration of Clinton Killian, filed March 20, 2024, complying with C.C.P. section 2015.5, no later than June 4, 2024. Failure to materially comply with any of the Court's directives will result in terminating sanctions at the hearing on June 12, 2024. Petitioner's counsel shall file a declaration no later than June 7, 2024 informing the Court whether Respondents have materially complied with the Court's directives above. If Respondents materially comply, the June 12, 2024 hearing will go off calendar. If not, the Court will hold the hearing with the intent to grant Petitioner's request for terminating sanctions. Petitioner Windy Cherry shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE HARRY GLASSMAN LIVING TRUST DATED APRIL 17, 2006, as Amended and Restated
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions, Specifically For Order: 1) Approving Settlement Agreement; 2) Reserving Jurisdiction To Enforce Settlement Agreement Under Ccp 664.6; And 3) For Such Other Relief As The Probate Court Deems Just And Proper Pursuant To Its Equitable Powers
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine if the settlement agreement should be approved despite the information missing in the petition and if the Court should retain jurisdiction to enforce the settlement agreement under CCP Sect. 664.6. The petition does not include a summary of the main terms of the settlement agreement or the application of the relevant substantive legal standard for approval to the facts presented in the petition showing that the legal standard has been met, as required by LRSF 14.43 D. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MONSERRATE MAGOBET
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/16/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The will and the testator's signature show that her first name was MONSERRATE. (2) The will was signed on 1/23/21, not 12/3/21, according to the attestation by the witnesses. (3) The notice showed what appears to be the incorrect first name of the decedent, the incorrect date of this hearing, and did not have a copy of the instructions for how to appear remotely attached to it. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) If the grandson, Domingo Magobet, post-deceased, who is his successor-in-interest? Did he die intestate or did he have a will or a trust? An amended petition must be filed to correct the date of the will and the decedent's first name if it was, in fact, Monserrate, not Monserrato. After the amended petition is filed, notice of the amended petition must be mailed and published. Please provide a new proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/5/24. If these issues cannot be addressed in time to provide the new proposed order and courtesy copies to the examiner by 7/5/24, Counsel should contact the examiner to request more time. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIE FRANCIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/30/24. (No earlier availability.) The following issues posted for the hearing on 5/7/24 still have not been addressed: (1) Attachment 3e(3) to the petition is unclear. Did the other daughter, Denise Riley, die in 2011 or 2017? (2) Both the wife and the other daughter post-deceased, so they (or, now, their estates) have an interest in this estate. Who are their successors-in-interest? Has anyone been appointed for their estates or did they have trusts? (3) If there are no assets of any value, why is this proceeding necessary? Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/19/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A new proof of service was filed on 5/6/24, still claiming that notice was mailed on 4/17/24 for the hearing on 5/7/24 but with the instructions for how to appear remotely attached to it. That seems unlikely if the instructions WERE attached to the notice mailed on 4/17/24, the verification of the proof of service filed on 5/6/24 should have been made more recently.)

rulings.Case Title: THE LIONEL WEST TRUST ESTABLISHED SEPTEMBER 16, 2014, AMENDED FEBRUARY 22, 2016
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Establishing Trust'S Claim Of Ownership Of Property And For Authorizing And Directing Transfer To Terrellyn West-Taylor, Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/30/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) A courtesy copy of the petition was not provided to the examiner, but the trust attached to the petition reviewed online was not actually signed by the settlor. (4) The petitioner lives in Maryland. [Probate Code Section 17002(a).] Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/19/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PHILIP J. WALLACE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing Status of closing the estate will be set for 6/24/2025. The administrator must file either petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before that status date. Appearance will be required on 6/24/2025 if no petition has been filed.
